faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Tremont apartment?
faqs.answer: Signs include small irregular holes in wool, cashmere, or silk clothing, silky tubes or cases in closets, web-like material in fabric folds, and small tan or brown moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice larvae (small white caterpillars) on clothing or in dark corners of closets. Our Tremont technicians provide free inspections to confirm infestations.

faqs.question: What factors affect the cost of clothes moth control in Tremont?
faqs.answer: Pricing ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on infestation severity, property size, number of affected rooms, and treatment method required. A single closet treatment costs less than whole-apartment fumigation. We provide upfront estimates after inspecting your Tremont home and customize solutions to your budget and needs.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take in a Tremont home?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically takes 1-3 hours depending on your property size and infestation extent. Most Tremont residents see significant reduction within 1-2 weeks, with complete elimination in 4-6 weeks. Follow-up visits may be recommended to ensure all life stages are eliminated and prevent re-infestation.

faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use targeted treatments that are safe for humans, pets, and delicate fabrics when applied correctly.
